items.title: Velocity from position
items.scenario: A drone's height is h(t) = -4.9t² + 20t meters. The derivative h′(t) = -9.8t + 20 gives instantaneous vertical velocity at time t.
items.explanation: At t = 2 s, h′(2) = -9.8(2) + 20 = 0.4 m/s upward—much more informative than average speed over an interval.

items.title: Learning rate in 1D loss minimization
items.scenario: A single weight w has loss L(w) = (w - 3)². The derivative L′(w) = 2(w - 3) points uphill; gradient descent uses w ← w - η·L′(w) to move toward w = 3.
items.explanation: This is the scalar version of the update rule used in every neural network trainer—derivative tells direction and steepness.

items.title: Sensitivity of sigmoid output
items.scenario: A logistic unit outputs σ(x) = 1/(1+e^{-x}). Its derivative σ′(x) = σ(x)(1-σ(x)) is largest near x = 0 and small when saturated near 0 or 1.
items.explanation: Vanishing derivatives in saturated regions slow learning—why initialization and activation choice matter in deep networks.
